intro.title: Grammar of Graphics
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Grammar of Graphics is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode:
library(ggplot2)
print("Data + aesthetics + geoms + scales + theme")

challenges.title: Easy: Use ggplot2
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `ggplot2` related to **Grammar of Graphics**.

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: ggplot scatter
challenges.promptMarkdown: Load ggplot2 and create a scatter plot of `mpg` vs `wt` from `mtcars`.

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `ggplot2` and `ggplot2` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **Grammar of Graphics** in a short script using `ggplot2`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **Grammar of Graphics** (include `ggplot2`).

questions.prompt: What best describes Grammar of Graphics?

choices.label: A core R concept for data analysis

questions.prompt: Which function prints output in R?
R is widely used for:
What assignment operator is preferred in R?
Missing values in R are represented by:
Which function combines values into a vector?
Data frames are:
Type the function to get help on a topic.
Expert: Vectorized operations in R:
Expert: Name one keyword from Grammar of Graphics.

intro.title: Saving Plots
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Saving Plots is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode:
library(ggplot2)
p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point()
# ggsave("plot.png", p, width = 6, height = 4)

challenges.title: Easy: Use ggsave
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `ggsave` related to **Saving Plots**.

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: ggplot scatter
challenges.promptMarkdown: Load ggplot2 and create a scatter plot of `mpg` vs `wt` from `mtcars`.

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `ggsave` and `ggsave` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.
